name: America the Beautiful — Military Lifetime Pass
description: Free lifetime entrance pass to 2,000+ federal recreation sites including national parks, national wildlife refuges, national forests, and BLM lands. Covers entrance and standard amenity fees for the pass holder and passengers in a single, private, non-commercial vehicle.
eligibility: All US military veterans with DD-214. Gold Star Families also eligible.
verification: DD-214, military ID, VHIC, or veteran designation on state-issued ID. Free at any federal recreation site; $10 for online/mail order.

name: Military Annual Pass (Active Duty)
description: Free annual pass for current active duty military members and their dependents. Covers entrance fees at national parks, forests, wildlife refuges, and other federal lands.
eligibility: Current US military active duty, Reserve, and National Guard with valid military ID (CAC). Dependents with valid military dependent ID also eligible.
verification: Common Access Card (CAC) or military dependent ID. Free at any federal recreation site or via USGS online store.

name: Access Pass (Disabled Veterans)
description: Free lifetime pass for veterans with a permanent disability (service-connected or not). Provides free entrance to federal recreation sites plus 50% discount on some amenity fees such as camping, swimming, and boat launching.
eligibility: US citizens with permanent disabilities. Includes veterans with any permanent service-connected disability.
verification: Documentation of permanent disability such as VA disability letter, SSDI award, or statement from licensed physician.

name: BLM Public Lands — Free Veteran Access
description: Free lifetime access to over 245 million acres of Bureau of Land Management public lands for recreation including hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, off-roading, and rock climbing.
eligibility: All US military veterans. Covered under the America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass.
verification: America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass or Access Pass.

name: US Army Corps of Engineers — Free Day Use
description: Free day-use access at Army Corps of Engineers recreation areas nationwide. Over 400 lake and river projects with campgrounds, beaches, hiking trails, and boat ramps.
eligibility: All US military veterans and Gold Star Families. Covered under the America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass.
verification: America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass.

name: US Fish & Wildlife Service — Free Refuge Access
description: Free entrance to 560+ National Wildlife Refuges for hiking, wildlife viewing, photography, fishing, and hunting. Covered by the America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass.
eligibility: All US military veterans and Gold Star Families.
verification: America the Beautiful Military Lifetime Pass or Access Pass.

name: Recreation.gov — Federal Campground Reservations
description: Central reservation system for federal campgrounds, cabins, day-use areas, and permits across National Parks, National Forests, BLM, and Corps of Engineers sites. Veterans with the Access Pass receive 50% off camping fees.
eligibility: Open to all; disabled veterans with Access Pass receive 50% discount on amenity fees.
verification: Access Pass displayed at campground. Online booking may require calling the reservation line to apply discount.

name: California Distinguished Veteran Pass
description: Free vehicle day use, camping, and boat use at over 100 California State Parks. One of the most comprehensive state park veteran benefits in the country.
eligibility: California resident veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability OR former POWs OR Medal of Honor recipients.
verification: Apply via California Department of Parks and Recreation with VA disability letter and CA residency proof.

name: Texas Veterans Parklands Passport
description: Free entry to all Texas state parks for honorably discharged veterans with 60%+ VA disability rating. Waives daily entrance fee ($2–$7 per adult) for the pass holder.
eligibility: Texas resident veterans with 60%+ service-connected disability. Must have honorable or general discharge.
verification: Apply at any Texas state park or by mail with DD-214 and VA disability letter.

name: Florida State Parks — Veteran Discounts
description: 25% discount on annual entrance passes for all veterans. Free lifetime military pass for veterans with service-connected disabilities. Florida has 175 state parks.
eligibility: All Florida resident honorably discharged veterans (25% off). Free for service-connected disabled veterans.
verification: Military ID, DD-214, or veteran designation on FL driver license at any state park entrance.

name: New York Lifetime Liberty Pass
description: Free or discounted access to New York state parks, historic sites, and recreational facilities for veterans and Gold Star Families.
eligibility: New York resident veterans with honorable discharge. Gold Star Families also eligible.
verification: Apply online or at any state park with DD-214 and NY residency proof.

name: Washington Lifetime Disabled Veteran Pass
description: Free day-use parking, moorage, boat launch, and overnight camping at all Washington state parks for disabled veterans.
eligibility: Washington resident veterans with 30%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply at parks.wa.gov with VA disability letter and WA residency proof.

name: Oregon Disabled Veteran Outdoor Recreation Pass
description: Free camping and day-use parking at Oregon state parks for service-connected disabled veterans. One of the few states offering free camping for veterans.
eligibility: Oregon resident veterans with any service-connected disability (any rating).
verification: Apply at any Oregon state park or online with VA disability letter.

name: Minnesota Free State Park Vehicle Permit (Disabled Veterans)
description: Free year-round unlimited access to 75 Minnesota state parks for veterans with service-connected disabilities.
eligibility: Minnesota resident veterans with any service-connected disability.
verification: Apply via Minnesota DNR with VA disability documentation.
annual_value: $35 vehicle permit saved plus daily fees

name: Colorado — Free Lifetime Hunting & Fishing License
description: Free lifetime combination small game, fishing, and fur bearer license for qualified disabled veterans and Purple Heart recipients.
eligibility: Colorado resident veterans with 60%+ service-connected disability OR Purple Heart recipients.
verification: Apply through Colorado Parks and Wildlife with VA disability letter or Purple Heart documentation.

name: Illinois — Free Veteran Hunting & Fishing License
description: Free hunting and fishing license for veterans receiving compensation for service-connected disability of 10% or more, or total disability.
eligibility: Illinois resident veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability compensation or 100% total disability.
verification: Apply through Illinois Department of Natural Resources with VA benefit verification letter.

name: Idaho — Disabled American Veteran Hunt/Fish License
description: Reduced-fee combination hunting and fishing license for disabled veterans. Idaho also offers a free big game tag for veterans with 40%+ disability.
eligibility: Idaho resident veterans with 40%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply through Idaho Fish & Game with VA disability documentation.

name: New Jersey — Free Disabled Veteran Hunting & Fishing
description: Free resident hunting, fishing, and trapping licenses plus all permits and stamps for veterans with any service-connected disability.
eligibility: New Jersey resident veterans with any service-connected disability (any rating).
verification: Apply through NJ Division of Fish and Wildlife with VA disability letter and NJ residency proof.
annual_value: $40–$100+ in license and permit fees saved

name: Georgia — One-Time Veterans Hunting & Fishing License
description: Free one-year hunting and fishing license for honorably discharged Georgia veterans who served 90+ days on active duty. Available once per lifetime.
eligibility: Georgia resident veterans with 90+ days active duty and honorable discharge. One-time benefit.
verification: Apply through Georgia Department of Natural Resources with DD-214.

name: Utah — Discounted Disabled Veteran Hunting & Fishing
description: Discounted combination hunting and fishing license for resident veterans with 20%+ service-connected disability.
eligibility: Utah resident veterans with 20%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply through Utah Division of Wildlife Resources with VA disability documentation.

name: Kansas — Free Lifetime Hunting & Fishing License
description: Free permanent resident combination hunting and fishing license for Kansas veterans with 30%+ service-connected disability.
eligibility: Kansas resident veterans with 30%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply through Kansas Department of Wildlife & Parks with VA disability letter.

name: Louisiana — Free Lifetime Combo License
description: Free lifetime combination basic hunting and fishing license for Louisiana veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability.
eligibility: Louisiana resident veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply through Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries with VA disability documentation.

name: Oregon — Free Disabled Veteran Hunting & Fishing
description: Free resident and non-resident hunting and fishing permits for disabled veterans in Oregon. Additional to standard license requirement.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability (resident or non-resident).
verification: Apply through Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife with VA disability documentation.

name: Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's — Legendary Salute Military Discount
description: 10% off most items (5% on firearms and ammunition) at all Bass Pro Shops and Cabela's locations. The largest outdoor retailer military discount program in the US.
eligibility: Active duty, veterans, retired military, and law enforcement with valid ID.
verification: Military ID, DD-214, or ID.me verification at checkout. Available in-store and online.

name: Vail Resorts — Military Epic Pass
description: Heavily discounted Epic Pass for military. Active duty/retired: $200. Veterans/adult dependents: $640. Covers 40+ resorts including Vail, Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Park City, and Whistler.
eligibility: Active duty, retired military, veterans, and their dependents. Verification required.
verification: GovX ID verification required during purchase. One pass per verified individual.

name: Ikon Pass — Military Pricing
description: Discounted Ikon Pass and Ikon Base Pass pricing for military members. Covers 50+ ski destinations including Aspen, Jackson Hole, Mammoth, Steamboat, and Big Sky.
eligibility: Active duty, retired military, veterans, Reserve, and National Guard with valid military verification.
verification: GovX or ID.me verification during purchase.

name: Big Bear Mountain Resort — Military Lift Ticket Discount
description: 25% off window rate on single-day lift tickets at Big Bear Mountain Resort and Snow Summit in Southern California.
eligibility: Active duty military, veterans, and military dependents with valid ID.
verification: Military ID presented at ticket window.

name: KOA Campgrounds — Military Discount
description: 10% off nightly camping rates at participating KOA campgrounds nationwide. KOA operates 500+ campgrounds across North America.
eligibility: Active duty military, veterans, and military families with valid military ID.
verification: Present military ID at check-in. Discount varies by location and season; call ahead to confirm.

name: Sierra Club Military Outdoors
description: Free outdoor trips and adventures for veterans and military families, including hiking, camping, rafting, kayaking, and rock climbing. Operates across 11 regional chapters with scholarships for outdoor employment training.
eligibility: All veterans, active duty, Reserve, National Guard, and military family members. No disability requirement.
verification: Self-identification as veteran or military family member during registration.

name: Wounded Warrior Project — Soldier Ride & Outdoor Adventures
description: Free multi-day cycling events, adaptive sports, skiing, snowboarding, and outdoor adventure programs for wounded, ill, and injured veterans.
eligibility: Post-9/11 veterans with service-connected injuries or illnesses. Active registration with WWP required.
verification: Register at woundedwarriorproject.org. Must be post-9/11 era and have service-connected condition.

name: Team Rubicon — Disaster Response & Outdoor Service
description: Free disaster response operations and outdoor service projects. All meals, lodging, transportation, and equipment provided during deployments. Veterans gain purpose, community, and outdoor experience.
eligibility: All veterans, active duty, and civilian volunteers. No disability requirement.
verification: Sign up at teamrubiconusa.org. Complete online training modules before first deployment.

name: Wounded Warrior Outdoors
description: Free outdoor adventure programs specifically designed for wounded and disabled veterans, including hunting, fishing, camping, and wilderness expeditions.
eligibility: Wounded or disabled veterans. Programs tailored to various ability levels.
verification: Apply via woundedwarrioroutdoors.org with veteran and disability documentation.

name: Higher Ground — Adaptive Outdoor Sports
description: Adaptive skiing, snowboarding, cycling, paddling, and outdoor programs for veterans with physical disabilities and cognitive injuries. Professional adaptive instructors and equipment provided free of charge.
eligibility: Veterans with physical disabilities, TBI, PTSD, or other service-connected conditions.
verification: Apply at highergroundusa.org. Must provide documentation of disability or service-connected condition.

name: Warriors and Quiet Waters — Fly Fishing
description: Free multi-day fly fishing retreats in Montana for post-9/11 combat veterans. Includes lodging, meals, equipment, guided fishing, and peer support.
eligibility: Post-9/11 combat veterans with honorable or general discharge.
verification: Apply at warriorsandquietwaters.org. Selection via application review.

name: Project Healing Waters — Fly Fishing for Disabled Vets
description: Free fly fishing and outdoor education programs for disabled active military and veterans. Operates in 200+ locations nationwide with volunteer guides.
eligibility: Disabled active duty military and veterans at VA hospitals and military bases.
verification: Programs offered through VA medical centers and military installations. Self-referral or clinician referral.

name: Killington/Pico — Military Express Card
description: Up to 50% off lift tickets for military. Free skiing on Veterans Day and Memorial Day at Killington and Pico resorts in Vermont.
eligibility: Active duty, veterans, retired military, and military dependents.
verification: Military ID at ticket window. Express Card may be purchased for season-long discounts.

name: Grand Targhee Resort — Military Discount
description: 30% discount on lift tickets for military at Grand Targhee Resort in Wyoming. One of the steepest military discounts in western skiing.
eligibility: Active duty, veterans, retired military, and spouses with valid military ID.
verification: Military ID at ticket window.

name: Maine — Free Veteran Hunting, Fishing & Trapping
description: Free hunting, fishing, trapping, and guide license for Maine resident veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability.
eligibility: Maine resident veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability.
verification: Apply through Maine Department of Inland Fisheries and Wildlife with VA disability documentation.
